overview.value: Monstera is a tropical climbing houseplant known for its split leaves. It grows best with steady warmth, bright filtered light, and a chunky, well-draining potting mix.

careDifficultyDescription.value: Forgiving once light and watering are balanced, but sensitive to soggy soil.

light.value: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh afternoon sun.

water.value: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil feels dry, usually every 7-10 days indoors.

humidityDescription.value: Prefers moderate to high humidity, but adapts to average indoor air.

diseaseRiskDescription.value: Overwatering can cause yellow leaves, root rot, and fungus gnats.

fertilizer.value: Feed monthly during spring and summer with a balanced diluted fertilizer.

tips.value: Give it a moss pole or support if you want larger, more mature leaves.
